mission.title: Install and Configure Rook Ceph Operator
mission.description: Rook Ceph Operator is an open-source cloud-native storage orchestrator for Kubernetes that automates the deployment and management of Ceph storage. It provides self-managing, self-scaling, and self-healing storage services, making it ideal for large-scale production environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of Ceph and the need for proper resource allocation.

steps.title: Add Rook Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Rook Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Rook from the official source.

```bash

helm repo add rook-release https://charts.rook.io/release

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Rook Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Rook CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Rook to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rook/rook/v1.19.4/deploy/cluster/examples/kubernetes/ceph/cluster.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs for Rook.

steps.title: Install Rook Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Rook operator using Helm, which manages the lifecycle of the Ceph cluster.

```bash

helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --create-namespace --version v1.19.4

```

> Installs the Rook Ceph operator in the rook-ceph namespace.
commands: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --create-namespace --version v1.19.4

steps.title: Create Ceph Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Ceph cluster by applying the cluster configuration YAML file.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rook/rook/v1.19.4/deploy/examples/ceph/cluster.yaml

```

> This command creates the Ceph cluster.

steps.title: Verify Rook Ceph Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Rook Ceph cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-ceph get pod

kubectl -n rook-ceph get cephcluster

```

> This command lists all pods in the rook-ceph namespace to verify their status.
> This command checks the status of the Ceph cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rook Ceph Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Rook Ceph operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will lead to data loss if persistent volumes are not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph

kubectl delete namespace rook-ceph

```

> Uninstalls the Rook Ceph operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rook Ceph Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade the Rook Ceph operator to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --version v1.19.4

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl -n rook-ceph get pod

```

> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrades the Rook Ceph operator.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --version v1.19.4

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create ceph cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error may occur due to insufficient resources or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your nodes meet the minimum resource requirements and check the configuration files for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Pod in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Ceph operator is unable to start due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n rook-ceph' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Ceph cluster status shows 'HEALTH_WARN'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are issues within the Ceph cluster that need to be addressed.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl -n rook-ceph exec -it <ceph-tools-pod> -- ceph health detail' to get more information on the warnings.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no available monitors'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Ceph monitors are not running or reachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the monitor pods and ensure they are running and healthy.

versionNotes.changes: Improved stability and performance of Ceph cluster management, added support for new storage classes.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl -n rook-ceph get pod' showing all pods in the 'Running' state and 'kubectl -n rook-ceph get cephcluster' showing 'HEALTH_OK'.
codeSnippets: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-ceph --create-namespace --version v1.19.4

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
